category_title: Students with aggressive behaviors and impulse control difficulties who connect through animal-related interests
minimal_context: Dominic is playing with blocks during free choice time and needs to transition to cleanup.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Free choice time is over, it is time to clean up the blocks.

STUDENT: (Takes materials away from another child) No.

TUTOR: We need to start cleaning up now.

STUDENT: (Hits the other child) I am playing with these.

TUTOR: Please put the blocks away.

STUDENT: (Becomes frustrated and throws the blocks) No clean up!

options.A: Closely observe the student's interests and current understandings each day, and then build activities for the next day or week based on those observations.
options.B: Implement an in-class reward system where the student earns a sticker of his favorite animal for his chart whenever he successfully transitions to cleanup.
options.C: Adhere to an established set of rules and penalties regarding behavior, starting with a warning, followed by a timeout, and then increasingly serious disciplinary measures.
options.D: Model appropriate behaviors during daily routines and transitions using puppets to demonstrate how to clean up the blocks and interact with peers.
